title: A CASE label column
prompt_md:
APIs frequently return a human-friendly label derived from a raw value, rather than making the frontend re-implement the rule. For every movie, return its `title` and a computed `tier` label using a `CASE` expression:

- `rating >= 8.0` -> `'Top Rated'`
- otherwise       -> `'Good'`

Return columns `title` and `tier`.
hints: `CASE WHEN <condition> THEN <value> ELSE <value> END` is SQL's if/else.
Alias the whole CASE expression with `AS tier`.
reference_solution: SELECT title, CASE WHEN rating >= 8.0 THEN 'Top Rated' ELSE 'Good' END AS tier FROM movies;
